selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to have a robust fact-checking mechanism in place. This can include using AI-powered tools to detect and flag suspicious content, as well as partnering with reputable third-party fact-checkers to verify the accuracy of posts. Additionally, social media platforms should prioritize transparency by providing clear guidelines on what constitutes fake content and how it will be addressed. By taking a proactive approach to addressing fake content, social media platforms can help restore trust among their users and promote a safer online environment.",
"reasoning_summary": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, a robust fact-checking mechanism is crucial. This involves using AI-powered tools to detect suspicious content and partnering with third-party fact-checkers to verify its accuracy. Additionally, social media platforms must prioritize transparency by providing clear guidelines on what constitutes fake content and how it will be addressed.",
"tone": "informative",

}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to have a robust fact-checking mechanism in place. This can include using AI-powered tools to detect and flag suspicious content, as well as partnering with reputable third-party fact-checkers to verify the accuracy of posts. Additionally, social media platforms should prioritize transparency by providing clear guidelines on what constitutes fake content and how it will be addressed. By taking a proactive approach to addressing fake content, social media platforms can help restore trust among their users and promote a safer online environment.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, a robust fact-checking mechanism is crucial. This involves using AI-powered tools to detect suspicious content and partnering with third-party fact-checkers to verify its accuracy. Additionally, social media platforms must prioritize transparency by providing clear guidelines on what constitutes fake content and how it will be addressed.
